points.text: Yarrow Bioscience is developing YB-101, a first-in-class anti-thyroid stimulating hormone receptor (TSHR) monoclonal antibody targeting Graves' disease and thyroid eye disease.

AbbVie acquired Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9 billion to secure zumilokibart, an antibody for atopic dermatitis, asthma, and eosinophilic esophagitis, expanding its monoclonal antibody portfolio beyond Humira and Skyrizi.

GSK purchased Nuvalent for $10.6 billion to advance its oncology pipeline, complementing its existing development of the antibody-drug conjugate risvutatug rezetecan and its recent acquisition of monoclonal antibody developer RAPT Therapeutics.
Incyte agreed to acquire Star Therapeutics for $1.25 billion to gain VGA039, a monoclonal antibody for von Willebrand disease currently in Phase 3 trials with multiple FDA designations.

Teva Pharmaceuticals and Polpharma Biologics signed a global licensing agreement for a biosimilar candidate to Ocrevus (ocrelizumab), a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting CD20-positive B cells for multiple sclerosis.

FUJIFILM Biotechnologies — Holly Springs facility — Monoclonal antibody (mAb) manufacturing — Selected for FDA PreCheck Pilot Program to accelerate regulatory review and strengthen U.S. supply chain resilience.
Sanofi received US approval for Sarclisa Escena (isatuximab), a subcutaneous anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody formulation administered via the CirCLIQ® on-body injector for multiple myeloma treatment.

Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. — TEV-'408 — Vitiligo — Advancing anti-interleukin-15 monoclonal antibody into Phase 2b in Q4 2026 following Phase 1b data showing 75% of patients reported facial vitiligo improvement.

Polpharma granted Teva exclusive rights to commercialize IV and SC formulations of a biosimilar candidate to Ocrevus (ocrelizumab), a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting CD20-positive B cells for multiple sclerosis, upon regulatory approval.

VYNE Therapeutics — Merger with Yarrow Bioscience — Corporate Transaction — Declared special cash dividend of $16.5M ($0.38/share) pending closing of merger to acquire Yarrow, developer of YB-101 anti-TSHR monoclonal antibody.

AbbVie — Apogee Therapeutics Acquisition — Monoclonal Antibodies (zumilokibart) — $10.9 billion deal secures zumilokibart for atopic dermatitis, asthma, and eosinophilic esophagitis; marks AbbVie's first acquisition of 2026.

GSK — Nuvalent Acquisition — Oncology (zidesamtinib, risvutatug rezetecan) — $10.6 billion purchase adds zidesamtinib for NSCLC and solid tumors; reinforces pipeline alongside antibody-drug conjugate risvutatug rezetecan and prior RAPT Therapeutics mAb acquisition.

Incyte — Star Therapeutics Acquisition — von Willebrand Disease (VGA039) — $1.25 billion deal for Phase 3 monoclonal antibody VGA039; includes up to $750 million in sales milestones; candidate holds fast track, orphan drug, and breakthrough therapy designations.

Teva / Polpharma Biologics — Ocrevus Biosimilar — Multiple Sclerosis (ocrelizumab) — Global licensing agreement for biosimilar candidate to humanized monoclonal antibody ocrelizumab; Teva handles regulatory submissions and commercialization in US, EU, and other key markets.

FUJIFILM Biotechnologies — Holly Springs site — Monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) — FDA PreCheck Pilot Program participant; site manufactures mAbs for clients including argenx, Johnson & Johnson, and Regeneron.

Sanofi — Sarclisa Escena (isatuximab) — Multiple Myeloma — US FDA approved subcutaneous formulation administered via CirCLIQ® on-body injector, marking first anticancer treatment delivered via this device.

VYNE Therapeutics / Yarrow Bioscience — YB-101 — Graves' Disease / Thyroid Eye Disease — Proposed merger includes clinical-stage anti-TSHR monoclonal antibody program; special cash dividend of $16.5M declared pending closing.
Teva — TEV-'408 — Vitiligo — Phase 1b open-label study: 42% achieved F-VASI50, 21% achieved F-VASI75, 55% reported total body vitiligo improvement; Phase 2b initiation planned Q4 2026.
AbbVie — Apogee Therapeutics — Atopic dermatitis/Asthma — $10.9 billion acquisition securing zumilokibart (monoclonal antibody) and pipeline; first US pharma acquisition of 2026.
Incyte — Star Therapeutics — von Willebrand disease — $1.25 billion acquisition of VGA039 (monoclonal antibody) in Phase 3; deal includes up to $750M in sales milestones.
GSK — RAPT Therapeutics — Monoclonal antibody development — Acquisition of developer to expand biologics portfolio alongside ADC risvutatug rezetecan for non-small cell lung cancer.

Polpharma — Biosimilar to Ocrevus (ocrelizumab) — Multiple Sclerosis — Exclusive licensing deal signed with Teva for commercialization of IV and SC formulations across US, EU, Brazil, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Israel, and Turkey; Polpharma retains development and manufacturing lead.

Yarrow Bioscience — Development of first-in-class anti-TSHR monoclonal antibody — YB-101 — Clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on autoimmune thyroid diseases; targeted for acquisition by VYNE Therapeutics.

AbbVie — Monoclonal antibody portfolio expansion — zumilokibart (via Apogee) — $10.9 billion acquisition completed; adds antibody assets for atopic dermatitis, asthma, and eosinophilic esophagitis to existing Humira and Skyrizi franchise.

GSK — Oncology and monoclonal antibody pipeline growth — zidesamtinib, risvutatug rezetecan — $10.6 billion Nuvalent acquisition; complements existing antibody-drug conjugate risvutatug rezetecan and prior RAPT Therapeutics mAb developer buyout.

Incyte — Bleeding disorders portfolio expansion — VGA039 — $1.25 billion acquisition of Star Therapeutics; secures Phase 3 monoclonal antibody for von Willebrand disease with multiple FDA designations.

Teva Pharmaceuticals — Biosimilars commercialization strategy — Ocrevus biosimilar (ocrelizumab) — Global licensing deal with Polpharma Biologics; leverages Teva's commercial footprint for humanized monoclonal antibody biosimilar in multiple sclerosis.

FUJIFILM Biotechnologies — U.S. manufacturing expansion — Monoclonal antibody production — $3.2B Holly Springs site (8 x 20,000L bioreactors) accelerating expansion to add 8 x 20,000L capacity by late FY2027.
Sanofi — Commercialization of subcutaneous monoclonal antibody delivery — Sarclisa Escena (isatuximab) — US approval secured for on-body injector administration in multiple myeloma.

Yarrow Bioscience — Development of first-in-class TSHR-targeting therapy — YB-101 (anti-thyroid stimulating hormone receptor monoclonal antibody) — Clinical-stage asset central to merger with VYNE Therapeutics.
Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. — Immunology pipeline expansion — TEV-'408 (anti-IL-15 mAb) — Phase 2b initiation in vitiligo; quarterly subcutaneous dosing design.
AbbVie — Strategic M&A for autoimmune assets — Apogee Therapeutics (zumilokibart mAb) — $10.9B acquisition; complements Humira and Skyrizi monoclonal antibody franchise.
Incyte — Bleeding disorders portfolio expansion — Star Therapeutics (VGA039 mAb) — $1.25B acquisition; VGA039 targets von Willebrand disease in Phase 3.
GSK — Oncology and biologics pipeline growth — RAPT Therapeutics (monoclonal antibody developer) — Acquisition announced June 2026; complements ADC risvutatug rezetecan.

Teva — Commercialization of CD20-targeting monoclonal antibody biosimilar — Ocrevus (ocrelizumab) biosimilar — Secured exclusive rights from Polpharma for global rollout pending regulatory approval; Polpharma leads manufacturing.

Polpharma — Biosimilar development and manufacturing — Ocrevus (ocrelizumab) biosimilar — Partnered with Teva for global commercialization; retains lead role in development and manufacturing of the humanized monoclonal antibody candidate.

YB-101 — Mechanism: Anti-thyroid stimulating hormone receptor (TSHR) monoclonal antibody — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Graves' disease and thyroid eye disease — Mechanism: Designed to directly disrupt central mechanism of autoimmune thyroid diseases.

zumilokibart — Mechanism: Antibody targeting atopic dermatitis, asthma, and eosinophilic esophagitis — Phase: Not specified (acquired asset) — Population: Patients with atopic dermatitis, asthma, and eosinophilic esophagitis — Comparator: N/A — Status: Acquired by AbbVie for $10.9 billion.

VGA039 — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody for von Willebrand disease — Phase: 3 — Population: Patients with von Willebrand disease (affects ~1% of population) — Comparator: Takeda's Vonvendi (implied competitor) — Endpoints/Results: Holds fast track, orphan drug, rare pediatric disease, and breakthrough therapy designations from FDA.

ocrelizumab (biosimilar candidate) — Mechanism: Humanized monoclonal antibody targeting CD20-positive B cells — Phase: Pre-approval (biosimilar development) — Population: Relapsing forms and primary progressive multiple sclerosis — Comparator: Ocrevus (ocrelizumab) — Endpoints/Results: Global licensing agreement signed for commercialization upon regulatory approval.

YB-101 — Mechanism: Anti-thyroid stimulating hormone receptor (TSHR) monoclonal antibody — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Graves' disease and thyroid eye disease — Mechanism: Direct disruption of central disease pathway.
Sarclisa Escena (isatuximab) — Mechanism: Anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody — Formulation: Subcutaneous — Delivery: CirCLIQ® on-body injector — Indication: Multiple myeloma — Status: US approved.

TEV-'408 — Mechanism: Anti-interleukin-15 monoclonal antibody — Phase: 1b (completed) / 2b (planned) — Population: Active or stable non-segmental vitiligo — Endpoints: 75% facial improvement, 42% F-VASI50, 21% F-VASI75; quarterly subcutaneous dosing.

VGA039 — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody targeting von Willebrand disease — Phase: 3 — Population: Patients with inherited bleeding disorder (1% of population) — Status: FDA fast track, orphan drug, rare pediatric disease, breakthrough therapy designations.

zumilokibart — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody for atopic dermatitis/asthma — Phase: Pre-commercial (acquired by AbbVie) — Indication: Atopic dermatitis, asthma, eosinophilic esophagitis — Status: Acquired via $10.9B Apogee deal.

Ocrelizumab biosimilar — Mechanism: Humanized monoclonal antibody targeting CD20-positive B cells — Phase: Pre-approval (biosimilar development) — Population: Multiple sclerosis patients — Comparator: Reference product Ocrevus — Status: Exclusive licensing deal signed for IV and SC formulations.
VYNE Therapeutics / Yarrow Bioscience — Proposed merger — Acquisition of Yarrow Bioscience — Special cash dividend of $16.5M declared; closing expected July 24, 2026 subject to stockholder approval.
AbbVie — Acquired Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9 billion; deal includes zumilokibart antibody assets for atopic dermatitis, asthma, and eosinophilic esophagitis; first acquisition of 2026.
GSK — Acquired Nuvalent for $10.6 billion; adds zidesamtinib for NSCLC and solid tumors; complements existing antibody-drug conjugate risvutatug rezetecan and prior RAPT Therapeutics mAb acquisition.
Incyte — Acquired Star Therapeutics for $1.25 billion; includes up to $750 million in sales milestones for VGA039, a Phase 3 monoclonal antibody for von Willebrand disease.

Teva / Polpharma Biologics — Signed global licensing agreement for ocrelizumab biosimilar; Polpharma retains development/manufacturing, Teva handles regulatory submissions and commercialization in US, EU, Brazil, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Israel, and Turkey.

FUJIFILM Biotechnologies — Manufacturing capacity expansion — Monoclonal antibody production — Accelerating drug substance capacity doubling (adding 8 x 20,000L bioreactors) by 6 months to target late FY2027.
VYNE Therapeutics — Proposed merger with Yarrow Bioscience — Acquisition of YB-101 monoclonal antibody program — $16.5M special cash dividend declared; closing expected July 24, 2026.
AbbVie — Acquired Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9 billion; secures zumilokibart (monoclonal antibody) and pipeline for autoimmune indications.
Incyte — Acquired Star Therapeutics for $1.25 billion; gains VGA039 (monoclonal antibody) for von Willebrand disease with up to $750 million in potential sales milestones.
GSK — Acquired RAPT Therapeutics (monoclonal antibody developer) to expand biologics capabilities; deal announced June 2026.

Polpharma / Teva — Exclusive licensing agreement — Ocrevus (ocrelizumab) biosimilar — Polpharma granted Teva exclusive commercialization rights for IV and SC formulations in US, EU, Brazil, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Israel, and Turkey; deal value undisclosed.
